stories.epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
stories.user_story: As an admin, I want SSO and role-based access, so that only authorised staff see sensitive comms.
stories.acceptance_criteria: SAML/OIDC SSO and SCIM provisioning (e.g. via WorkOS/Entra/Okta); granular roles; access audited.

stories.user_story: As an architect, I want autoscaling, decoupled GPU inference, so that batch audits and peak loads are handled without throttling the app tier.
stories.acceptance_criteria: GPU inference autoscaled on a dedicated tier (off Vercel/Render); queue + batch; latency SLA targets; per-analysis cost tracked and capped.

stories.epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
stories.user_story: As a security engineer, I want India/EU data residency and tenant isolation, so that client data stays compliant and separate.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Region-pinned storage and processing (India and/or EU per tenant); per-tenant isolation; AES-256 at rest, TLS1.2+ in transit; managed keys.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want to define and reuse named audience segments (employees, leadership, clients, press, regulators) with attributes such as role, region, language and seniority, so that every prediction is framed against the actual readers.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Create/edit/delete and reuse named audiences with role/region/language/seniority; selectable per analysis; a default set seeded per organisation; stored per tenant.

stories.user_story: As an admin, I want a configurable taxonomy of communication artifact types with engineering artifacts excluded, so that the analyser only ever scores in-scope comms.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Artifact types configurable per tenant; code/engineering documents blocked or flagged out-of-scope; selected type drives which checks run.

stories.user_story: As an admin, I want one central, versioned library of brand, tone, terminology and compliance rules, so that every analysis draws on a single source of truth.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Versioned rule library; per-artifact-type rule sets; publish/rollback; changes apply org-wide on publish.

stories.epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
stories.user_story: As an architect, I want every model's licence checked against our commercial use, so that the analyser is legally shippable.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Per-model licence register; TRIBE v2 (CC BY-NC 4.0) used in production only under a Meta commercial licence, OR restricted to internal/eval with production inference routed to a commercially-usable model; CI gate blocks non-compliant models; legal sign-off recorded.

stories.epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
stories.user_story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want every score stamped with model+adapter+mapping+input hash, so that any result is reproducible and auditable.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Versions recorded on every result; re-run reproduces the score; changelog of model and mapping changes maintained.

stories.user_story: As a compliance officer, I want lawful processing of any personal data in artifacts, so that we meet DPDP 2023 and GDPR.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Processing basis/consent captured; PII detection with redaction option; data-subject-rights support; configurable retention.

stories.user_story: As an engineer, I want monitoring, alerting and audit logging, so that the service is reliable and auditable.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Metrics/logs/traces (OpenTelemetry/Sentry); uptime SLO with alerting; append-only audit of actions; no PII in telemetry.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want to upload Word, PDF and PowerPoint files, so that I can analyse the artifacts we actually produce.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Parse docx/pptx/pdf (python-docx/python-pptx/PyMuPDF) incl. text, layout and images; slide structure preserved; OCR for scanned PDFs; source files stored in S3/R2-compatible object storage.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want instant text-only scoring for emails and memos, so that everyday comms get feedback without a full analysis run.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Synchronous sub-2s scoring of clarity/tone/brand on pasted/typed text; no GPU/multimodal run required; one-click upgrade to full analysis.

stories.user_story: As a policy officer, I want readability and plain-language scoring on policies and training, so that employees can actually understand them.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Readability indices; long/passive/ambiguous-sentence flags; jargon list; reading grade vs target.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want undefined acronyms and off-list jargon flagged, so that messages are clear to every audience.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Detect undefined/inconsistent acronyms; flag banned jargon against the standards library; suggest plain alternatives.

stories.user_story: As a policy officer, I want inclusive-language checks on all comms, so that we avoid biased or exclusionary wording.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Flags non-inclusive terms with alternatives; configurable per region/culture.

stories.user_story: As an exec-office writer, I want to tag each artifact's objective (inform, align, persuade, reassure, drive action), so that the composite score weights the metrics that matter for that intent.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Objective selectable per artifact; metric weighting (attention/emotion/comprehension/memory) changes by objective; objective and weighting shown in the report.

stories.user_story: As an internal-comms lead, I want scannability (headings, length, bullets) assessed, so that busy employees can skim.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Structure score; long-block/missing-heading flags; suggested chunking; tuned per channel.

stories.epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
stories.user_story: As an owner, I want a contractual and technical no-training-on-our-content guarantee, so that confidential comms aren't used to train shared models.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Zero-retention/no-train option; per-tenant model boundaries; enforced and auditable; reflected in contract.

stories.user_story: As a brand manager, I want to codify our tone-of-voice, approved/avoided terminology and core narratives in the platform, so that artifacts are scored against our actual standards rather than generic norms.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Brand-voice attributes, do/don't terminology and messaging pillars captured, versioned and applied during scoring; editable only by Brand Manager/Admin roles.

stories.epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
stories.user_story: As a brand manager, I want each artifact scored against codified brand voice, so that everything sounds like us.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Brand-alignment score; deviations with reasons and fixes; per-section; trend over time.

stories.user_story: As a brand manager, I want detected tone compared to our target tone, so that exec comms feel on-brand.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Tone profile (e.g. confident/empathetic/formal); gap vs target tone; sentence-level tone flags.

stories.epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
stories.user_story: As an architect, I want a model-agnostic interface with TRIBE v2 as primary and other models addable, so that we are never locked to one model or licence.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Common inference interface; route per metric/modality; add/swap/disable models by config; swapping the underlying model requires no application rewrite.

stories.epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
stories.user_story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want TRIBE v2 served on a decoupled GPU inference tier and called by the app, so that rendered artifacts yield audience-response signals with no model training.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Artifact rendered to TRIBE v2's expected modalities; inference runs on a GPU host (e.g. Modal/Replicate/Baseten), not on Vercel/Render; raw outputs captured per artifact/segment; no in-house training.

stories.epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
stories.user_story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want raw model signals mapped and calibrated into comms metrics, so that the analyser reports attention/comprehension/memorability, not raw vectors.
stories.benchmark_source: TRIBE v2 (raw response proxy), Neurons, DAIVID
stories.acceptance_criteria: Mapping converts raw outputs to 0-100 comms metrics; calibrated, documented and validated against human ground-truth; norms/embeddings in Postgres+pgvector. This layer is the analyser's core IP.

stories.user_story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want continuous validation against human ground-truth, so that we can state accuracy credibly.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Held-out human tests; accuracy reported per metric; drift monitoring; published methodology.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want a predicted-attention heatmap over a slide or email, so that I can see what readers notice first.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Saliency heatmap overlay; focus order; area-of-interest scoring; export as PNG.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want one headline effectiveness score, so that I can judge readiness at a glance.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Weighted composite 0-100 with attention/emotion/comprehension/memory/brand sub-scores; objective-weighted; pass/needs-work threshold.

stories.user_story: As an exec-office writer, I want the analyser inside Word and PowerPoint, so that I can analyse without leaving my tools.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Office.js add-ins analyse the current doc/deck; inline results; SSO; works in the client's M365 tenant.

stories.user_story: As an architect, I want multi-tenant isolation with per-tenant config, so that we can serve multiple IT-services clients securely from one codebase.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Tenant isolation; per-tenant brand/audience/standards config; usage metering; admin separation; same container images usable for single-tenant/on-prem.

stories.epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
stories.user_story: As a security engineer, I want PII in uploaded comms detected and optionally redacted, so that sensitive data is protected during analysis.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Detect PII; redact/mask option; configurable per tenant; action logged; PII never written to logs.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want to auto-detect and exclude engineering artifacts (code, configs, architecture/design docs), so that analysis stays within communication scope.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Classifier flags engineering/code content; excluded from scoring with a stated reason; admin-only override; decision logged.

stories.user_story: As an admin, I want model documentation and human-review controls, so that AI outputs are governed.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Model card (scope/data/limits) per model; human review required for high-stakes outputs; overrides logged.

stories.user_story: As a compliance officer, I want an append-only log of analyses, gate overrides and AI rewrites, so that governance is provable.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Append-only audit (actor/time/artifact/action/override reason); exportable; no PII in logs.

stories.user_story: As an owner, I want an org-level dashboard of comms effectiveness over time, so that I can manage the programme on data.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Trends by score, channel, team and artifact type; cohort views; export.

stories.user_story: As an admin, I want to queue large back-catalogue audits, so that we can baseline existing comms.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Bulk job queue (Redis-backed) on background workers; progress/status; partial results; completion notification.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager in a global IT firm, I want to analyse comms in English plus priority languages (incl. Hindi/Indic scripts), so that regional audiences are properly served.
stories.benchmark_source: TRIBE v2 (zero-shot languages), Poppulo (30+ langs), VisibleThread
stories.acceptance_criteria: Language auto-detect; analysis in English + configured priority languages; per-language norms; correct Indic-script handling in UI and exports.

stories.user_story: As an internal-comms lead, I want to analyse a published intranet page or email HTML in context, so that the as-seen version is assessed.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Render URL/HTML to image+DOM; capture above-the-fold; honour auth via connector; snapshot stored; no personal data placed in URLs.

stories.user_story: As a brand manager, I want a read on visual hierarchy and clutter on decks, so that slides aren't overloaded.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Clutter/complexity score; competing-focal-point flags; per-slide; benchmarked against strong decks.

stories.epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want consistency checks across a campaign or document set, so that messaging doesn't contradict itself.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Cross-document terminology/claims consistency; conflict flags; campaign-level view.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want benchmarks against our own best comms and artifact-type norms, so that scores are meaningful.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Percentile vs org history and artifact-type norm; top-quartile threshold; norms store grows with usage (pgvector).

stories.user_story: As an exec-office writer, I want to compare two versions side by side, so that I pick the stronger one before sending.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Side-by-side scores and deltas; highlights what changed; recommends a winner with reasons.

stories.user_story: As an internal-comms lead, I want pre-send analysis in Outlook and Teams, so that internal messages are checked before they go out.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Office.js add-ins run a pre-send check; warn or block on low score (configurable); one-click full report.

stories.user_story: As a platform engineer at the client, I want a documented API and SDK, so that we can embed analysis into our own workflows and DAM.
stories.acceptance_criteria: REST API with OpenAPI spec + SDK; API-key auth; rate limits; webhooks for async results; versioned docs.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want draft->review->approve stages with sign-off, so that exec comms are governed.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Configurable stages; reviewers; comments; sign-off; status tracked; full audit trail.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want a shareable analysis report, so that I can brief stakeholders.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Branded PDF/HTML report (scores, heatmaps, recommendations); access-controlled share link; export stored in object storage.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want each recommendation to cite its driver, so that I can trust and act on the advice.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Each recommendation links to the metric and segment evidence behind it; expected uplift shown; accept/track.

stories.user_story: As an internal-comms lead, I want guidance tuned to the channel (email, intranet, town-hall deck, press release), so that advice fits the medium.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Channel profiles with format rules; channel-aware scoring and tips; subject-line/preview checks for email.

stories.epic: E17 Security, Privacy, IP & Data Residency
stories.user_story: As a security engineer at a regulated client, I want a private or on-prem deployment, so that confidential exec and board comms never leave our perimeter.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Containerised (Docker) images deployable to client VPC/Kubernetes/air-gapped; no data egress; customer-managed keys; documented runbook.

stories.user_story: As an internal-comms lead, I want to upload town-hall recordings and exec videos, so that spoken communications are analysed too.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Accept mp4/mov/wav/mp3; transcribe via self-hosted Whisper (residency/no-train safe) with timestamps; align transcript to frames; run as an async job with status; media stored in object storage.

stories.epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
stories.user_story: As an ML/platform engineer, I want to combine specialised models per metric alongside TRIBE v2, so that each metric uses the best-fit model.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Per-metric model assignment (e.g. saliency for attention, emotion model, NLP for clarity); results merged under one scoring contract; configurable per artifact type; all behind the abstraction layer.

stories.epic: E03 Model Orchestration & Inference Layer (TRIBE v2 + open models)
stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want scores per slide, paragraph or scene, so that I can see exactly which parts work and which don't.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Per-slide/paragraph/scene scoring; timeline view for audio/video; drill-down to weakest segments.

stories.user_story: As an exec-office writer, I want to know what lands in the first seconds and above the fold, so that the key message is never missed.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Early-attention score; flag when key message/brand falls outside a high-salience zone; recommendation to reposition.

stories.user_story: As an internal-comms lead, I want predicted emotional response (e.g. trust, reassurance, anxiety) to a message, so that sensitive comms land as intended.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Emotion spectrum per artifact/segment with intensity and valence; flags unintended negative emotion; per-segment for AV.

stories.user_story: As a policy officer, I want change, restructuring and crisis comms checked for fear or ambiguous framing, so that we communicate responsibly.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Detects alarming/ambiguous framing; suggests clearer, reassuring alternatives; review recorded.

stories.user_story: As a training lead, I want a predicted cognitive-load and comprehension score, so that training materials aren't overwhelming.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Cognitive-demand score per section; overload flags; chunking suggestions; benchmarked.

stories.user_story: As an exec-office writer, I want to confirm the core message is clearly stated and easy to find, so that the point isn't buried.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Identify the stated key message; score prominence and clarity; flag if buried; suggest a stronger lead.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want a predicted memorability score for the key message, so that important comms stick.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Predicted recall for key message and overall; weak-retention flags; second-by-second for AV.

stories.epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
stories.user_story: As an exec-office writer, I want live brand and clarity guidance while I write in Word/PowerPoint, so that I fix issues at the source.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Office sidebar flags brand/tone/clarity inline; one-click fixes; score updates live.

stories.epic: E08 Brand Voice, Tone & Consistency Governance
stories.user_story: As an admin, I want AI-generated drafts scored and guardrailed before they enter comms, so that AI content stays on-brand and compliant.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Scores LLM output against the standards library; blocks off-brand/non-compliant content; offers fixes; action logged.

stories.user_story: As a policy officer, I want risky or over-promising language flagged in press releases and policies, so that we limit legal and regulatory exposure.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Detects unsupported claims/risk phrases; tied to the policy library; routed to a review queue.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want to see why a score is what it is, so that I trust and act on it.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Drivers per score; segment attributions; plain-language explanation; no black box.

stories.user_story: As a policy officer, I want the models checked for bias across languages and regions, so that predictions are fair.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Bias tests across segments; disparity reporting; mitigation log; per-model model card.

stories.user_story: As an admin, I want minimum scores per artifact type before publish, so that sub-standard comms are caught.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Configurable thresholds; block or warn; override requires a logged reason; per artifact type.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want specific recommendations for weak segments, so that I know how to fix them, not just what's wrong.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Prioritised fixes per weakness with expected uplift and rationale; accept/dismiss.

stories.user_story: As an architect, I want a pluggable connector framework (M365, Google, DAM, CMS), so that the analyser fits existing comms stacks.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Pluggable connectors with per-connector auth and permissions; SharePoint/Teams/Drive first; certified M365/Google.

stories.user_story: As an internal-comms lead, I want to comment on and assign fixes within an analysed artifact, so that the team improves it together.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Threaded comments; assign and resolve; @mentions; notifications.

stories.user_story: As an admin, I want per-team and per-author quality trends, so that I can target coaching.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Scorecards per team/author; improvement trend; privacy-aware aggregation (no surveillance framing).

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want subject lines and opening hooks scored and improved, so that messages get opened and read.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Generate and score subject/hook variants; predicted open/read; select and insert.

stories.user_story: As an exec-office writer, I want a deck analysed as a timed viewing sequence rather than just text, so that pacing and flow are assessed.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Deck rendered (LibreOffice headless) to a timed visual+text sequence; per-slide and flow scores; pacing/overload flags; async job.

stories.user_story: As an exec-office writer, I want delivery cues (pace, emphasis, filler) in exec videos surfaced, so that leaders present with impact.
stories.acceptance_criteria: From transcript+audio: pace, filler and emphasis cues with suggestions; no biometric data on the speaker stored without consent.

stories.user_story: As an exec-office writer, I want to see which points are most and least memorable, so that I can reinforce the right ones.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Points ranked by predicted retention; repetition/placement suggestions; exportable.

stories.user_story: As a brand manager, I want to benchmark our public comms against peers, so that we stand out.
stories.benchmark_source: Dragonfly (Chrome ext competitor analysis), System1 league tables
stories.acceptance_criteria: Scores public peer comms; relative positioning and gap insights; respects copyright/ToS.

stories.user_story: As an internal-comms lead, I want a predicted open/read estimate before sending, so that I can improve reach.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Predicted open/read range vs norms with drivers; improve-then-recheck loop.

stories.user_story: As an owner, I want predicted scores correlated with actual engagement, so that I can prove value.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Joins predicted scores to downstream engagement (opens/read/sentiment); correlation report with stated caveats.

stories.user_story: As an exec-office writer, I want on-brand AI rewrites of weak passages, so that I can improve copy fast without losing voice.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Rewrites honour brand voice and compliance rules; before/after predicted score shown; human approval required before use.

stories.user_story: As a comms manager, I want to preview the predicted score if I accept changes, so that I can justify edits.
stories.acceptance_criteria: Edited draft re-scored on demand; uplift vs baseline shown; decision logged.
